L1.area: Audio feature extraction and rhythm analysis

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled string parameter is copied into a fixed-size stack buffer using an unbounded string copy operation without length validation, allowing a crafted input to overwrite adjacent stack memory.

L3.area: Tempo detection module initialization and method string parsing
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the tempo detection module where a user-supplied method name string is copied into an undersized stack buffer using an unbounded string copy operation during object initialization. If the method string exceeds the buffer capacity, it overwrites adjacent stack memory, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: In new_aubio_tempo, the tempo_mode parameter is copied into a 20-byte stack buffer using strcpy without bounds checking, causing a heap/stack overflow when the input exceeds 19 characters.
